ai_summary: Here's an executive summary of the document:  Bilcare Limited has received a National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) order condoning its significant delays in repaying public fixed deposits totaling an initial Rs. 171.28 crores. The company is now directed to deposit the remaining outstanding balance (approximately Rs. 2.52 to Rs. 3.25 crores plus accrued interest) into the government's Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF) by July 15, 2026, shifting the claim process for unpaid depositors to the IEPF. While this materially resolves a long-standing liability for Bilcare, it highlights persistent past financial distress, including its "relief undertaking" status, and presents a minor discrepancy in the reported final outstanding amount as a red flag.
